questions.question: 1 (a) Circuit A Close the switch. Measure, and record in Table 2.1, the potential difference (p.d.) V across resistor U. Measure, and record in Table 2.1, the current I in the circuit. Open the switch. Table 2.1 circuitV / V I / A R / Ω A B C
questions.text: 1 (a) Circuit A Close the switch. Measure, and record in Table 2.1, the potential difference (p.d.) V across resistor U. Measure, and record in Table 2.1, the current I in the circuit. Open the switch. Table 2.1 circuitV / V I / A R / Ω A B C

parts.answer: correct calculations o f b / a to 2 or more decimal places  axes labelled with quantity and unit  appropriate scales (plots occupying at least ½ grid between plotted points)  plots all correct to ½ small square and precise plots  well- j udged line and thin line G present and triangle method seen on graph M R in range 100 to 200 and with unit of g
b calculated from b = 48.7  p 0625/53 May/June 2023

V all to at least 1 d.p. and I all to at least 2 d.p. correct calculation of R for circuit B consistent 2 or 3 significant figures for R all within 10% of each othe r statement matching results within limits of experimental accuracy / owtte with values / comparative values seen can obtain continuous set of values / more straightforward to change current / can obtain more values easily rectangle with strike-through arrow only 0625/53 May/June 2023  = 15° ± 1° separation sufficiently large

first set of lines and pin positions present and complete second set of lines present in correct relation to first set  = 25(°) ± 3(°) statement matching results j ustification matching statement and quoting values one suitable precaution, e.g.:  thin lines / sharp pencil  view bottom / base of pins / keep pins upright / at right angles  ensure pins far apart

difficult to align pins / place pins accurately / pins (too) thick 0625/53 May/June 2023 4 MP1 apparatus: thermometer and stop-watch MP2 method: measure independent variable and measure temperature and time MP3 repeat for new value of independent variable MP4 control variable: any one from:  volume of water  initial temperature MP5 table: columns, with units, for independent variable, temperature, time MP6 analysis: compare readings to see if change in lid thickness produces change in (rate of) temperature change (owtte) plot line graph (with axes specified) MP7 additional point any one from:  at least 5 sets of data taken  repeat for each value of independent variable and take average  nd appropriate control variable stated  description of calculating rate of cooling from results
